pattern: Each character in the string is replaced by the character k positions later in the same string, wrapping around cyclically using modulo.
hints: For position i, the replacement character is s[(i + k) % len(s)] — you look k positions forward in the string, not in the alphabet.
Because k can be larger than the string length, reduce k modulo n first to avoid redundant cycling.
Build the result by collecting s[(i + k % n) % n] for each index i.

edge_inputs.description: k equals string length — every char maps to itself
k larger than string length — wrap using modulo
k is a multiple of string length — identity mapping
length 100 string, k=1 — shift by one

stress.description: random lowercase strings with random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(chr(rng.randint(ord('a'), ord('z'))) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    return [s, k]
